title.en: Unlocking LLM Code Correction with Iterative Feedback Loops
title.zh: 通过迭代反馈循环解锁LLM代码修正能力

abstract: Large Language Models have shown remarkable capabilities in code generation. However, most existing evaluations focus only on single-attempt accuracy and overlook the iterative refinement process that is central to real-world programming. This study presents a systematic investigation of LLMs' ability to rectify their own code through execution feedback. Using real-world programming problems across four models and two major programming languages, this study evaluates performance using iterative refinement framework where LLMs receive compiler error messages and testcase feedback after each attempt. This study introduces metrics to evaluate code failures, analyze rectification patterns, and compare the effectiveness of reasoning and non-reasoning models, offering actionable insights into both the understanding and practical application of feedback loops in LLM-driven code generation systems. Results show that reasoning models consistently improve over iterations, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback, while syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures.

relevance.en: This paper extends your interest in execution-feedback-driven training by systematically evaluating LLMs' iterative code correction with compiler/test feedback, and introduces metrics (ISR@k) that move beyond isolated pass@k, though it focuses on single-file problems rather than repository-level or long-context synthesis.
relevance.zh: 本文通过系统性地评估LLM在编译器/测试反馈下的迭代代码修正，扩展了您对执行反馈驱动训练的兴趣，并引入了超越孤立pass@k的指标（ISR@k），尽管它关注的是单文件问题而非仓库级或长上下文合成。

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.zh: 使用DeepSeek-R1进行迭代次数限制校准。

figures.reference_text: Iteration Limit Setup To balance performance and computational cost, we determined an appropriate iteration limit via a preliminary experiment on 10 randomly sampled problems from the Challenge Dataset. Figure 2 showed that success rates plateaued after the 8th iteration. Accounting for marginal cases, we set a maximum of 10 iterations per problem.

figures.label: Figure 3
figures.reference_text: Experimental Procedure: As demonstrated in Figure 3, the iterative frame- work uses a multi-turn mechanism: each iteration involves (1) sending a prompt, (2) generating a solution, and (3) receiving execution feedback. The initial prompt follows the baseline structure (Section 2.3). Subsequent prompts include all pre- vious solutions and feedback, giving the model full context. Iterations continue until a correct solution is obtained or the 10th iteration is reached. All solutions and results were stored for analysis using metrics from Section 2.5.

figures.label: Figure 4
figures.reference_text: Execution Feedback LeetCode categorizes results as Accepted, Compile Er- ror, Runtime Error, Wrong Answer, Time Limit Exceeded, and Memory Limit Exceeded. Each error type can typically be corrected in specific ways (e.g., Time Limit Exceeded via algorithmic optimization). Execution feedback is provided to guide LLMs toward effective corrections, framing the feedback as actionable guidance in the form of instructive prompts (Figure 4).

figures.label: Figure 5

caption.en: Example of multi-turn conversation with LLM

figures.reference_text: Prompt Design A standard multi-turn JSON-based prompt format is adopted (Figure 5). Prompts are labeled as “user” and model responses as “assistant”. In each iteration, the LLM accesses the full conversation history, enabling it to leverage prior solutions and instructive feedback to generate improved outputs over successive iterations.

figure.label: Figure 2

caption.zh: 使用DeepSeek-R1进行迭代次数限制校准。

figure.reference_text: Iteration Limit Setup To balance performance and computational cost, we determined an appropriate iteration limit via a preliminary experiment on 10 randomly sampled problems from the Challenge Dataset. Figure 2 showed that success rates plateaued after the 8th iteration. Accounting for marginal cases, we set a maximum of 10 iterations per problem.

brief_summary.en:
Most evaluations of large language models (LLMs) for code generation focus on single-attempt accuracy, ignoring the iterative refinement central to real-world programming. This leaves a critical gap: we don't know how well LLMs can correct their own code using execution feedback like compiler errors and test results. This paper systematically investigates that process by introducing an iterative refinement framework where LLMs repeatedly generate solutions, receive feedback, and improve over multiple rounds. The authors propose new metrics—such as Iterative Success Rate at k—and analyze rectification patterns across four models and two programming languages.

The results reveal that reasoning models (e.g., DeepSeek-R1) consistently improve with each iteration, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models, which plateau after minimal gains. Syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures, suggesting that automated feedback loops should target lower-level errors while humans focus on high-level logic. Overall, this work moves beyond single-shot benchmarks to provide actionable insights for designing multi-turn feedback workflows—especially the importance of choosing reasoning models and structuring feedback around error types for practical LLM-assisted coding.
brief_summary.zh:
大多数针对大型语言模型（LLM）代码生成的评估只关注单次尝试的准确率，忽略了实际编程中至关重要的迭代优化过程。这造成了一个关键缺口：我们不清楚LLM在利用编译器错误和测试结果等执行反馈来修正自身代码方面的能力。本文通过引入一个迭代优化框架来系统性地研究这一过程，在该框架中，LLM反复生成解决方案、接收反馈并在多轮中不断改进。作者提出了新的指标——例如第k次迭代成功率（Iterative Success Rate at k）——并分析了四种模型和两种编程语言下的修正模式。

结果表明，推理模型（如DeepSeek-R1）在每次迭代中持续改进，显著优于非推理模型，后者在获得微小提升后便停滞不前。语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理，这表明自动反馈循环应针对低级错误，而人类则专注于高级逻辑。总体而言，这项工作超越了单次基准测试，为设计多轮反馈工作流提供了可操作的见解——特别是选择推理模型以及根据错误类型构建反馈对于实际LLM辅助编码的重要性。

problem.en: Existing evaluations of large language models (LLMs) for code generation focus predominantly on single-attempt accuracy, neglecting the iterative refinement process that is central to real-world programming. This oversight creates a significant gap: it remains unclear how effectively LLMs can rectify their own code through execution feedback, such as compiler error messages and test case results. Without a systematic study, practitioners lack actionable insights into the mechanisms and limitations of LLM-driven code correction, particularly for distinguishing between models that can leverage feedback versus those that cannot.
problem.zh: 现有对大型语言模型（LLM）代码生成的评估主要关注单次尝试的准确率，忽视了实际编程中核心的迭代优化过程。这一疏忽造成了一个显著缺口：尚不清楚LLM如何有效地通过执行反馈（如编译器错误信息和测试用例结果）来修正自身代码。缺乏系统性研究，从业者无法获得关于LLM驱动代码修正机制和局限性的可操作见解，特别是在区分能够利用反馈的模型和不能利用反馈的模型方面。

solution.en: This paper presents a systematic investigation of LLMs' ability to correct their own code using iterative feedback loops. The key contribution is a framework that enables LLMs to refine solutions over multiple attempts by receiving compiler errors and test case feedback after each generation. The authors introduce novel metrics—such as Iterative Success Rate at k (ISR@k) and failure-type analysis—to evaluate and compare rectification patterns. A central insight is that reasoning models (e.g., DeepSeek-R1) significantly outperform non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback, while syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures. This work moves beyond single-shot benchmarks to provide a realistic assessment of LLMs' debugging capabilities.
solution.zh: 本文系统性地研究了LLM使用迭代反馈循环修正自身代码的能力。关键贡献是一个框架，使LLM能够在多次尝试中通过每次生成后接收编译器错误和测试用例反馈来优化解决方案。作者引入了新颖的指标——例如第k次迭代成功率（ISR@k）和失败类型分析——来评估和比较修正模式。一个核心见解是，推理模型（如DeepSeek-R1）在利用反馈方面显著优于非推理模型，而语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理。这项工作超越了单次基准测试，提供了对LLM调试能力的现实评估。

methodology.en: The iterative refinement framework operates as a multi-turn process: each iteration begins with a prompt (initial baseline or previous context), the LLM generates a solution, execution feedback (compiler error messages, run-time errors, test case outcomes) is provided, and the model uses full conversation history to produce an improved solution in the next turn. The iteration limit is set to 10, calibrated via a preliminary experiment on 10 random problems (Figure 2 shows success rates plateau after iteration 8). Four models are evaluated: two reasoning models (including DeepSeek-R1) and two non-reasoning models, across Python and Java. Two datasets are used: a Challenge Dataset (32 hard LeetCode problems selected for high failure rates) and a Strain Dataset (200 diverse programming problems). Prompts follow a JSON multi-turn format (Figure 5) where each exchange is labeled 'user' or 'assistant'. Execution feedback is categorized into Accepted, Compile Error, Runtime Error, Wrong Answer, Time Limit Exceeded, and Memory Limit Exceeded (Figure 4). Metrics include ISR@k and failure-type distributions. The framework compares baseline (single attempt) performance against iterative refinement (Figures 3 and 7). Additional experiments test the effect of instructive hints for Time Limit Exceeded errors (Figure 6).
methodology.zh: 迭代优化框架作为一个多轮过程运行：每次迭代从一个提示（初始基线或先前上下文）开始，LLM生成一个解决方案，提供执行反馈（编译器错误信息、运行时错误、测试用例结果），模型使用完整的对话历史在下一轮生成改进的解决方案。迭代次数限制设为10，通过一个初步实验在10个随机问题上校准（图2显示成功率在第8次迭代后趋于平稳）。评估了四种模型：两个推理模型（包括DeepSeek-R1）和两个非推理模型，涵盖Python和Java。使用了两个数据集：挑战数据集（32个高失败率的困难LeetCode问题）和压力数据集（200个多样化的编程问题）。提示遵循JSON多轮格式（图5），其中每次交换标记为“用户”或“助手”。执行反馈分类为：通过、编译错误、运行时错误、错误答案、超时、内存超限（图4）。指标包括ISR@k和失败类型分布。该框架比较了基线（单次尝试）性能与迭代优化（图3和图7）。额外实验测试了针对超时错误的指导性提示的效果（图6）。

experiments.en: Experiments are conducted on the Challenge Dataset (32 problems) and Strain Dataset (200 problems) using Python and Java. Baselines include single-attempt performance (no iteration) and the iterative framework with up to 10 attempts. Models evaluated: reasoning models (e.g., DeepSeek-R1) and non-reasoning models (e.g., GPT-3.5). Key results: Reasoning models consistently improve with iterations, while non-reasoning models show minimal gains. For example, cumulative ISR@10 for Python solutions (Figure 8) shows DeepSeek-R1 achieves ~80% success rate, whereas non-reasoning models plateau around 40%. Similar trends appear for Java (Figure 9). The overall success rate comparison (Figure 7) demonstrates that iterative framework boosts baseline success by 30–40 percentage points for reasoning models. Failure-type analysis reveals that syntactic and runtime errors are corrected more often than logical errors; for instance, adding a one-line suggestive hint for Time Limit Exceeded problems reduces TLE errors by 50% (Figure 6).
experiments.zh: 实验在挑战数据集（32个问题）和压力数据集（200个问题）上使用Python和Java进行。基线包括单次尝试性能（无迭代）和最多10次尝试的迭代框架。评估的模型：推理模型（如DeepSeek-R1）和非推理模型（如GPT-3.5）。关键结果：推理模型在迭代中持续改进，而非推理模型提升甚微。例如，Python解决方案的累积ISR@10（图8）显示DeepSeek-R1达到约80%的成功率，而非推理模型停滞在约40%。Java也出现类似趋势（图9）。总体成功率比较（图7）表明，迭代框架将推理模型的基线成功率提高了30-40个百分点。失败类型分析显示，语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑错误更常被修正；例如，为超时问题添加一行提示性建议将TLE错误减少了50%（图6）。

conclusion.en: The study confirms that iterative feedback loops with execution signals significantly boost LLM code correction, with reasoning models far outperforming others by consistently improving across multiple attempts. Critically, syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures, suggesting that automated refinement should target lower-level errors while human oversight focuses on higher-level logic. For real-world programming, this means prioritizing reasoning models in multi-turn feedback workflows and designing human-AI collaboration to reserve human effort for complex logical debugging. However, the findings are limited to standalone LeetCode-style problems and modest iteration counts, so caution is needed when scaling to large codebases. Overall, the key takeaway is that structured, failure-type-aware feedback loops—not just single-shot accuracy—are essential for practical LLM-assisted coding.
conclusion.zh: The study confirms that iterative feedback loops with execution signals significantly boost LLM code correction, with reasoning models far outperforming others by consistently improving across multiple attempts. Critically, syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures, suggesting that automated refinement should target lower-level errors while human oversight focuses on higher-level logic. For real-world programming, this means prioritizing reasoning models in multi-turn feedback workflows and designing human-AI collaboration to reserve human effort for complex logical debugging. However, the findings are limited to standalone LeetCode-style problems and modest iteration counts, so caution is needed when scaling to large codebases. Overall, the key takeaway is that structured, failure-type-aware feedback loops—not just single-shot accuracy—are essential for practical LLM-assisted coding.

question.en: How does the iterative refinement framework introduced in this paper leverage execution feedback (compiler errors and test case results) to improve code correctness, and what implications does this have for integrating execution feedback into RL training for code generation?
question.zh: 本文引入的迭代优化框架如何利用执行反馈（编译器错误和测试用例结果）来提高代码正确性，这对将执行反馈整合到代码生成的RL训练中有什么启示？

reason.en: This ties directly to the user's interest in post-training techniques (RL with execution feedback) and examines how the framework's feedback loop can inform RL-based methods that leverage execution signals to improve code generation correctness.
reason.zh: 这直接关联到用户对后训练技术（带执行反馈的RL）的兴趣，并考察了该框架的反馈循环如何为基于执行信号改进代码生成正确性的RL方法提供信息。

question.en: What are the key differences in rectification patterns between reasoning and non-reasoning models when handling logical or algorithmic failures versus syntactic or runtime errors, and how do these insights guide the design of SFT or preference learning strategies for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: 在处理逻辑或算法失败与语法或运行时错误时，推理模型和非推理模型在修正模式上的关键差异是什么？这些见解如何指导仓库级代码生成的SFT或偏好学习策略的设计？

reason.en: This question aligns with the user's focus on SFT and preference learning, as understanding model-specific failure modes can inform targeted training approaches to improve correctness in complex tasks, including multi-file or repository-level code generation.
reason.zh: 这个问题与用户对SFT和偏好学习的关注一致，因为理解模型特定的失败模式可以为针对性的训练方法提供信息，以提高复杂任务（包括多文件或仓库级代码生成）的正确性。

question.en: How does the paper's iterative refinement methodology and metrics (e.g., failure analysis by error type) address the limitation of single-attempt evaluations like pass@k, and what recommendations does it offer for developing benchmarks that assess compilation, execution, and integration with existing codebases?
question.zh: 本文的迭代优化方法和指标（例如按错误类型进行失败分析）如何解决像pass@k这样的单次尝试评估的局限性？它为开发评估编译、执行以及与现有代码库集成的基准测试提供了哪些建议？

reason.en: This directly addresses the user's interest in advanced evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k, probing how the paper's approach can inspire benchmarks that measure real-world viability (compilation, execution, integration) in software engineering contexts.
reason.zh: 这直接涉及用户对超越pass@k的高级评估方法的兴趣，探讨了本文的方法如何激发衡量实际可行性（编译、执行、集成）的基准测试。

question.en: Given that the study shows reasoning models outperform non-reasoning models in leveraging execution feedback, what specific mechanisms or training paradigms (e.g., chain-of-thought, RLHF) might explain this advantage, and how can these be adapted to enhance repository-level code generation with multi-turn feedback?
question.zh: 鉴于研究表明推理模型在利用执行反馈方面优于非推理模型，哪些具体机制或训练范式（例如思维链、RLHF）可能解释这一优势？如何调整这些机制以增强具有多轮反馈的仓库级代码生成？

reason.en: This explores the user's interest in RLHF and post-training techniques, connecting the paper's findings on reasoning models to broader questions about improving code synthesis across multiple files or long-context tasks through iterative refinement.
reason.zh: 这探索了用户对RLHF和后训练技术的兴趣，将本文关于推理模型的发现与通过迭代优化改进跨多个文件或长上下文任务的代码合成联系起来。

question.en: How do the paper's results on iterative refinement across up to 10 attempts inform the design of human-in-the-loop systems for real-world software engineering tasks, and what implications does the tractability of different error types have for optimizing human-AI collaboration?
question.zh: 本文关于最多10次迭代优化的结果如何为实际软件工程任务中的人机协作系统设计提供信息？不同错误类型的可处理性对优化人机协作有什么启示？

reason.en: This aligns with the user's interest in human-in-the-loop evaluation and usage, using the paper's error-type analysis to suggest how iterative feedback loops can be standardized or adapted for practical, interactive code generation scenarios.
reason.zh: 这符合用户对人机协作评估和使用的兴趣，利用本文的错误类型分析来建议如何标准化或调整迭代反馈循环以适应实际的交互式代码生成场景。

question.en: How does the iterative refinement framework introduced in this paper leverage execution feedback (compiler errors and test case results) to improve code correctness, and what implications does this have for integrating execution feedback into RL training for code generation?
question.zh: 本文引入的迭代优化框架如何利用执行反馈（编译器错误和测试用例结果）来提高代码正确性，这对将执行反馈整合到代码生成的RL训练中有什么启示？

reason.en: This ties directly to the user's interest in post-training techniques (RL with execution feedback) and examines how the framework's feedback loop can inform RL-based methods that leverage execution signals to improve code generation correctness.
reason.zh: 这直接关联到用户对后训练技术（带执行反馈的RL）的兴趣，并考察了该框架的反馈循环如何为基于执行信号改进代码生成正确性的RL方法提供信息。

answer.en: The paper's iterative refinement framework directly leverages execution feedback by feeding compiler error messages and test case results back to the LLM after each code generation attempt, prompting it to iteratively correct its output. The abstract states that 'LLMs receive compiler error messages and testcase feedback after each attempt' in 'an iterative refinement framework,' and the study evaluates 'performance using iterative refinement framework where LLMs receive compiler error messages and testcase feedback after each attempt.' This feedback loop enables models to improve code correctness over multiple rounds: the authors report that 'reasoning models consistently improve over iterations, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback,' and that 'syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures.' These findings have direct implications for integrating execution feedback into RL training for code generation: they suggest that RL algorithms can be designed to reward models not just for final correctness but for learning to iteratively incorporate compiler and test signal—especially valuable for syntactic and runtime errors—while also highlighting that logical/algorithmic failures may require richer feedback or exploration strategies. The paper's explicit use of 'compiler error messages' and 'testcase feedback' as the feedback signals provides a concrete template for RL reward modeling, where the feedback loop itself becomes the training environment.
answer.zh: 本文的迭代优化框架直接利用执行反馈，在每次代码生成尝试后将编译器错误信息和测试用例结果反馈给LLM，促使其迭代修正输出。摘要指出，在“迭代优化框架”中，“LLM在每次尝试后接收编译器错误信息和测试用例反馈”，并且研究评估了“使用迭代优化框架的性能，其中LLM在每次尝试后接收编译器错误信息和测试用例反馈”。这种反馈循环使模型能够在多轮中提高代码正确性：作者报告称，“推理模型在迭代中持续改进，在利用反馈方面显著优于非推理模型”，并且“语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理”。这些发现对将执行反馈整合到代码生成的RL训练中具有直接启示：它们表明RL算法可以设计为不仅奖励最终正确性，还奖励学习迭代地整合编译器和测试信号——特别是对于语法和运行时错误——同时强调逻辑/算法失败可能需要更丰富的反馈或探索策略。本文明确使用“编译器错误信息”和“测试用例反馈”作为反馈信号，为RL奖励建模提供了具体模板，其中反馈循环本身成为训练环境。

question.en: What are the key differences in rectification patterns between reasoning and non-reasoning models when handling logical or algorithmic failures versus syntactic or runtime errors, and how do these insights guide the design of SFT or preference learning strategies for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: 在处理逻辑或算法失败与语法或运行时错误时，推理模型和非推理模型在修正模式上的关键差异是什么？这些见解如何指导仓库级代码生成的SFT或偏好学习策略的设计？

reason.en: This question aligns with the user's focus on SFT and preference learning, as understanding model-specific failure modes can inform targeted training approaches to improve correctness in complex tasks, including multi-file or repository-level code generation.
reason.zh: 这个问题与用户对SFT和偏好学习的关注一致，因为理解模型特定的失败模式可以为针对性的训练方法提供信息，以提高复杂任务（包括多文件或仓库级代码生成）的正确性。

answer.en: The paper finds a key difference in rectification patterns: "syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures" for both reasoning and non-reasoning models. However, reasoning models "consistently improve over iterations, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback." This suggests that while all models struggle more with logical/algorithmic faults, reasoning models are better at using feedback to correct those harder failures. For SFT or preference learning, these insights guide focusing training on logical/algorithmic errors rather than syntactic ones, and on iterative refinement loops that reward models which successfully leverage feedback, especially for complex tasks like repository-level code where logical consistency across files is critical. The paper itself is about single-file code correction, but the comparative failure-mode analysis directly informs design: SFT could expose models to examples of logical error correction, while preference learning could reward iterative improvement on harder failures.
answer.zh: 本文发现修正模式的一个关键差异：“语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理”，这对推理模型和非推理模型都成立。然而，推理模型“在迭代中持续改进，在利用反馈方面显著优于非推理模型”。这表明，虽然所有模型在逻辑/算法故障上更挣扎，但推理模型更善于利用反馈来纠正那些更困难的失败。对于SFT或偏好学习，这些见解指导将训练重点放在逻辑/算法错误而非语法错误上，并放在迭代优化循环上，奖励那些成功利用反馈的模型，特别是对于仓库级代码等复杂任务，其中跨文件的逻辑一致性至关重要。本文本身是关于单文件代码修正的，但比较性的失败模式分析直接指导设计：SFT可以让模型接触逻辑错误修正的示例，而偏好学习可以奖励在更困难失败上的迭代改进。

question.en: How does the paper's iterative refinement methodology and metrics (e.g., failure analysis by error type) address the limitation of single-attempt evaluations like pass@k, and what recommendations does it offer for developing benchmarks that assess compilation, execution, and integration with existing codebases?
question.zh: 本文的迭代优化方法和指标（例如按错误类型进行失败分析）如何解决像pass@k这样的单次尝试评估的局限性？它为开发评估编译、执行以及与现有代码库集成的基准测试提供了哪些建议？

reason.en: This directly addresses the user's interest in advanced evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k, probing how the paper's approach can inspire benchmarks that measure real-world viability (compilation, execution, integration) in software engineering contexts.
reason.zh: 这直接涉及用户对超越pass@k的高级评估方法的兴趣，探讨了本文的方法如何激发衡量实际可行性（编译、执行、集成）的基准测试。

answer.en: The paper directly addresses the limitation of single-attempt evaluations like pass@k by emphasizing that "most existing evaluations focus only on single-attempt accuracy and overlook the iterative refinement process that is central to real-world programming." Its methodology employs an "iterative refinement framework where LLMs receive compiler error messages and testcase feedback after each attempt," and it introduces metrics to "evaluate code failures, analyze rectification patterns, and compare the effectiveness of reasoning and non-reasoning models." The failure analysis by error type is explicitly included: the paper states that "syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures," showing how error-type granularity reveals differential tractability across iterations. However, the paper does not offer specific recommendations for developing benchmarks that assess compilation, execution, and integration with existing codebases. Its focus is on evaluating iterative correction of standalone code snippets via execution feedback, not on designing benchmarks for integration with larger codebases. Thus, while it provides a robust iterative methodology and error-type diagnostics to surpass pass@k, it stops short of prescribing benchmark design for codebase integration.
answer.zh: 本文直接解决了像pass@k这样的单次尝试评估的局限性，强调“大多数现有评估只关注单次尝试的准确率，忽视了实际编程中核心的迭代优化过程”。其方法采用“迭代优化框架，其中LLM在每次尝试后接收编译器错误信息和测试用例反馈”，并引入指标来“评估代码失败、分析修正模式，并比较推理模型和非推理模型的有效性”。按错误类型进行失败分析被明确包含：论文指出“语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理”，显示了错误类型粒度如何揭示跨迭代的不同可处理性。然而，本文没有为开发评估编译、执行以及与现有代码库集成的基准测试提供具体建议。它的重点是评估通过执行反馈对独立代码片段进行迭代修正，而不是设计用于与更大代码库集成的基准测试。因此，虽然它提供了超越pass@k的稳健迭代方法和错误类型诊断，但并未规定代码库集成的基准测试设计。

question.en: Given that the study shows reasoning models outperform non-reasoning models in leveraging execution feedback, what specific mechanisms or training paradigms (e.g., chain-of-thought, RLHF) might explain this advantage, and how can these be adapted to enhance repository-level code generation with multi-turn feedback?
question.zh: 鉴于研究表明推理模型在利用执行反馈方面优于非推理模型，哪些具体机制或训练范式（例如思维链、RLHF）可能解释这一优势？如何调整这些机制以增强具有多轮反馈的仓库级代码生成？

reason.en: This explores the user's interest in RLHF and post-training techniques, connecting the paper's findings on reasoning models to broader questions about improving code synthesis across multiple files or long-context tasks through iterative refinement.
reason.zh: 这探索了用户对RLHF和后训练技术的兴趣，将本文关于推理模型的发现与通过迭代优化改进跨多个文件或长上下文任务的代码合成联系起来。

answer.en: The paper does not specify the training paradigms (e.g., chain-of-thought or RLHF) that explain the reasoning models' advantage, nor does it provide direct evidence for specific mechanisms. It only reports that "reasoning models consistently improve over iterations, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback" and that "syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures." The study focuses on iterative refinement using "compiler error messages and testcase feedback" but does not investigate how reasoning models' internal mechanisms (like chain-of-thought) or training methods (like RLHF) drive this difference. Therefore, it offers no actionable evidence on how to adapt these mechanisms to repository-level code generation with multi-turn feedback. The paper's scope is limited to evaluating correction patterns from execution feedback alone, not explaining or transferring the underlying training architecture.
answer.zh: 本文没有具体说明解释推理模型优势的训练范式（例如思维链或RLHF），也没有提供特定机制的直接证据。它只报告了“推理模型在迭代中持续改进，在利用反馈方面显著优于非推理模型”以及“语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理”。该研究专注于使用“编译器错误信息和测试用例反馈”进行迭代优化，但没有调查推理模型的内部机制（如思维链）或训练方法（如RLHF）如何驱动这种差异。因此，它没有提供关于如何调整这些机制以增强具有多轮反馈的仓库级代码生成的可操作证据。本文的范围仅限于评估来自执行反馈的修正模式，而不是解释或转移底层训练架构。

question.en: How do the paper's results on iterative refinement across up to 10 attempts inform the design of human-in-the-loop systems for real-world software engineering tasks, and what implications does the tractability of different error types have for optimizing human-AI collaboration?
question.zh: 本文关于最多10次迭代优化的结果如何为实际软件工程任务中的人机协作系统设计提供信息？不同错误类型的可处理性对优化人机协作有什么启示？

reason.en: This aligns with the user's interest in human-in-the-loop evaluation and usage, using the paper's error-type analysis to suggest how iterative feedback loops can be standardized or adapted for practical, interactive code generation scenarios.
reason.zh: 这符合用户对人机协作评估和使用的兴趣，利用本文的错误类型分析来建议如何标准化或调整迭代反馈循环以适应实际的交互式代码生成场景。

answer.en: The paper's iterative refinement framework—evaluating models across "up to 10 attempts" with compiler and test-case feedback—directly informs human-in-the-loop system design by demonstrating that multiple feedback loops can systematically improve code correctness, especially for reasoning models. The authors state that "reasoning models consistently improve over iterations, substantially outperforming non-reasoning models in leveraging feedback," suggesting that human-in-the-loop systems should prioritize deploying reasoning models and allocate human oversight to verify outputs after several iterations rather than intervening early. Furthermore, the finding that "syntactic and runtime errors are far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures" implies that in practical human-AI collaboration, humans should focus feedback on higher-level logic and algorithmic design, while automated tools can handle lower-level errors. This tractability distinction allows system designers to route error types: automate correction of syntactic/runtime issues via iterative execution feedback loops, and reserve human effort for resolving logical or algorithmic flaws that resist automated refinement, thus optimizing the cost-effectiveness of human-AI collaboration.
answer.zh: 本文的迭代优化框架——评估模型在“最多10次尝试”中带有编译器和测试用例反馈——直接为人机协作系统设计提供信息，通过展示多个反馈循环可以系统地提高代码正确性，特别是对于推理模型。作者指出“推理模型在迭代中持续改进，在利用反馈方面显著优于非推理模型”，这表明人机协作系统应优先部署推理模型，并将人工监督分配给在几次迭代后验证输出，而不是早期干预。此外，“语法错误和运行时错误比逻辑或算法失败更容易处理”这一发现意味着在实际人机协作中，人类应将反馈集中在更高级的逻辑和算法设计上，而自动化工具可以处理低级错误。这种可处理性区分允许系统设计者路由错误类型：通过迭代执行反馈循环自动化修正语法/运行时问题，并保留人工精力用于解决抵抗自动化优化的逻辑或算法缺陷，从而优化人机协作的成本效益。
